title: The Quran Said the Sun Is Moving. Science Said It Was Still. Guess Who Was Right.

sound.ambient: deep space cosmic ambience, low airy hum, vast and clean, subtle shimmer, no music, no melody, no instruments

_note: Quran-science miracle (36:38). Tight, flowing, Islamic-anchored, dramatic pauses, verse shown on screen (Arabic text only, no reciter audio = copyright-free). >60s. Voice: Oliver Silk.

segments.text: You were taught that the sun stands still… while the Earth circles around it. But fourteen centuries ago, the Quran said something the world would not understand for over a thousand years. It said the sun itself… is running.
segments.caption: The sun is running.

segments.arabic: وَالشَّمْسُ تَجْرِي لِمُسْتَقَرٍّ لَّهَا ۚ ذَٰلِكَ تَقْدِيرُ الْعَزِيزِ الْعَلِيمِ
segments.translation: And the sun runs toward its resting place. That is the decree of the Almighty, the All-Knowing.
segments.text: And the sun runs toward its resting place. That is the decree of the Almighty, the All-Knowing. Surah Ya-Sin, verse thirty-eight.
For most of history, mankind believed the sun was fixed in the sky. Motionless. Then we built telescopes… and discovered the truth.
segments.caption: We were wrong.

segments.text: Our sun is not still at all. It is racing through the galaxy at over eight hundred thousand kilometers an hour… carrying the Earth, and everything on it, along for the ride.
segments.caption: 800,000 km/h.

segments.text: It is circling the center of the Milky Way on a journey so vast… that a single orbit takes two hundred and thirty million years.
segments.caption: One orbit: 230 million years.

segments.text: But the verse said something deeper. The sun runs toward a resting place. A destination. An end.
segments.caption: Toward a resting place.

segments.sfx: deep low ominous impact, single hollow boom, dark
segments.text: And science agrees. One day, the sun will burn through the last of its fuel… and die.
segments.caption: The sun will die.

segments.text: So how did an unlettered man, in the middle of the desert, fourteen hundred years ago… know the sun was on a journey… with a final destination?
segments.caption: How did he know?

segments.text: The answer changes how you see everything. Follow Ketabi Studio, for the signs science is still catching up to.
segments.caption: Follow Ketabi Studio.

segments.text: Want more like this? Get Ketabi, free, at Ketabi Studio dot com.
segments.caption: Download Ketabi.

sources: Quran 36:38 (Surah Ya-Sin) - 'and the sun runs toward its resting place'
The sun orbits the galactic center at ~828,000 km/h; one orbit ~230 million years
Stellar evolution - the sun will exhaust its fuel and die (red giant -> white dwarf)
